questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: The vertices of a $\triangle A B C$ lies on a rectangular hyperbola such that the orthocentre of the triangle is $(3,2)$ and the asymptotes of the rectangular hyperbola are parallel to the coordinate axis. If the two perpendicular tangents of the hyperbola intersect at the point $(1,1)$Question: The equation of the asymptotes is
questions.solution: Perpendicular tangents intersect on the director circle of hyperbola and director circle of rectangular hyperbola is a point circle. Hence centre of hyperbola is (1, 1) and equation of asymptotes are $(\mathrm{x}-1)=0$ and $\mathrm{y}-1=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: The vertices of a $\triangle A B C$ lies on a rectangular hyperbola such that the orthocentre of the triangle is $(3,2)$ and the asymptotes of the rectangular hyperbola are parallel to the coordinate axis. If the two perpendicular tangents of the hyperbola intersect at the point $(1,1)$Question: Number of real normals that can drawn from the point $(1,1)$ to the rectangular hyperbola is
questions.solution: From the centre of hyperbola we can draw two real normals to the rectangular hyperbola
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: An asymptote to a curve is a straight line which tends to be become tangent when point of contact approaches to infinity. Let the equation of hyperbola is $\mathrm{S}=0$, then the equation of its asymptotes is $\mathrm{S}+\lambda=0$, which will be pair of lines. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The equation of hyperbola whose asymptotes are the lines $3 x-4 y+7=0$ and $4 x+3 y+1=0$ and which passes through point $(2,3)$ is -
questions.solution:
Equation of hyperbola whose asymptotes are $3 x-4 y+7=0$ and $4 x+3 y+1=0$ will be $(3 x-4 y+7)(4 x+3 y+1)+\lambda=0$
If it passes though $(2,3)$, then $\lambda=-18$
So hyperbola will be
$\begin{aligned}
& (3 x-4 y+7)(4 x+3 y+1)-18=0 \\
& 12 x^2-12 y^2-7 x y+31 x+17 y-11=0
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The difference between the second degree curve and pair of asymptotes is constant. If second degree curve represented by a hyperbola $S=0$, then the equation of its asymptotes is $S+\lambda=0$ where $\lambda$ is constant. Which will be a pair of straight lines, then we get $\lambda$. Then equation of asymptotes is $A \equiv S+\lambda=0$ and if equation of conjugate hyperbola of $S$ represented by $S_1$, then $A$ is the arithmetic mean of $S$ and $S_1$.Question: The asymptotes of a hyperbola having centre at the point $(1,2)$ are parallel to the lines $2 x+3 y=0$ and $3 x+2 y=0$. If the hyperbola passes through the point $(5,3)$, then its equation is
questions.solution:
Let the asymptotes be $2 x+3 y+\lambda=0$ and $3 x+2 y+\mu=0$
Since, asymptotes passes through (1, 2), then
$\lambda=-8 \text { and } \mu=-7$
Let the equation of hyperbola be
$(2 x+3 y-8)(3 x+2 y-7)+\gamma=0 \qquad...(i)$
$\because \quad$ It passes through $(5,3)$, then
$\begin{aligned}
& (10+9-8)(15+6-7)+\gamma=0 \\
& \Rightarrow 11 \times 14+\gamma=0 \\
& \therefore \quad \gamma=-154
\end{aligned}$

Putting the value of $\gamma$ in Eq. (i), then
$(2 x+3 y-8)(3 x+2 y-7)=154$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The difference between the second degree curve and pair of asymptotes is constant. If second degree curve represented by a hyperbola $S=0$, then the equation of its asymptotes is $S+\lambda=0$ where $\lambda$ is constant. Which will be a pair of straight lines, then we get $\lambda$. Then equation of asymptotes is $A \equiv S+\lambda=0$ and if equation of conjugate hyperbola of $S$ represented by $S_1$, then $A$ is the arithmetic mean of $S$ and $S_1$.Question: A hyperbola passing through origin has $3 x-4 y-1=0$ and $4 x-3 y-6=0$ as its asymptotes. Then the equation of its transverse and conjugate axes are
questions.solution:
The transverse axis is the bisector of the angle between asymptotes containing the origin and the conjugate axis is the other bisector. The bisectors of the angle between asymptotes are
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{(3 x-4 y-1)}{5}= \pm \frac{(4 x-3 y-6)}{5} \\
\Rightarrow & (3 x-4 y-1)= \pm(4 x-3 y-6) \\
\Rightarrow & x+y-5=0 \text { and } x-y-1=0
\end{aligned}$
Hence, transverse axis and conjugate axis are
$x+y-5=0 \text { and } x-y-1=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The difference between the second degree curve and pair of asymptotes is constant. If second degree curve represented by a hyperbola $S=0$, then the equation of its asymptotes is $S+\lambda=0$ where $\lambda$ is constant. Which will be a pair of straight lines, then we get $\lambda$. Then equation of asymptotes is $A \equiv S+\lambda=0$ and if equation of conjugate hyperbola of $S$ represented by $S_1$, then $A$ is the arithmetic mean of $S$ and $S_1$.Question: The tangent at any point of a hyperbola $16 x^2-25 y^2=400$ cuts off a triangle from the asymptotes and that the portion of it intercepted between the asymptotes, then the area of this triangle is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \because 16 x^2-25 y^2=400 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{x^2}{5^2}-\frac{y^2}{4^2}=1 \qquad...(i) \end{aligned}$ Let $\mathrm{P}(5 \sec \phi, 4 \tan \phi)$ be any point on the hyperbola (i) Equation of tangent at $P$ is $\frac{x}{5} \sec \phi-\frac{y}{4} \tan \phi=1 \qquad...(ii)$ And asymptotes of Eq. (i) are
$y= \pm \frac{4}{5} x \qquad...(iii)$ Solving Eqs. (ii) and (iii), then $\frac{x}{5} \sec \phi \mp \frac{x}{5} \tan \phi=1$ $\begin{aligned} \text{or} \quad x & =\frac{5}{(\sec \phi \mp \tan \phi)} \\ & =\frac{5(\sec \phi+\tan \phi)(\sec \phi-\tan \phi)}{(\sec \phi \mp \tan \phi)} \end{aligned}$ then we get $A \equiv[5(\sec \phi+\tan \phi), 4(\sec \phi+\tan \phi)]$ and $\mathrm{B} \equiv[(5(\sec \phi-\tan \phi),-4(\sec \phi-\tan \phi)]$ $\therefore$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$
$=\frac{1}{2}|-20-20|=20$ sq unit
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: The vertices of a $\triangle A B C$ lies on a rectangular hyperbola such that the orthocentre of the triangle is $(3,2)$ and the asymptotes of the rectangular hyperbola are parallel to the coordinate axis. If the two perpendicular tangents of the hyperbola intersect at the point $(1,1)$Question: Equation of the rectangular hyperbola is
questions.solution:
Equation of hyperbola is $x y-x-y+1+\lambda=0$
It passes through $(3,2)$ hence $\lambda=-2$
Equation of hyperbola is $x y=x+y+1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: An asymptote to a curve is a straight line which tends to be become tangent when point of contact approaches to infinity. Let the equation of hyperbola is $\mathrm{S}=0$, then the equation of its asymptotes is $\mathrm{S}+\lambda=0$, which will be pair of lines. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The centre of a rectangular hyperbola is $(1,2)$ and its asymptotes are parallel to lines $\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=2$ and $x-y=3$, then the equation of asymptotes are -
questions.solution:
Equation of lines parallel to the lines will be $x+y=\lambda$ and $x-y=\mu$ respectively. If these lines represents asymptotes then these lines must pass through the centre of the hyperbola i.e. $(1,2)$
so asymptotes will be
$x+y-3=0 \text { and } x-y+1=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: An asymptote to a curve is a straight line which tends to be become tangent when point of contact approaches to infinity. Let the equation of hyperbola is $\mathrm{S}=0$, then the equation of its asymptotes is $\mathrm{S}+\lambda=0$, which will be pair of lines. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The product of perpendiculars from any point on the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{16}-\frac{y^2}{25}=1$ to its asymptotes is equal to -
questions.solution:
Asymptotes of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{16}-\frac{y^2}{25}=1$ will be
$\frac{x^2}{16}-\frac{y^2}{25}=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{x}{4}+\frac{y}{5}\right)\left(\frac{x}{4}-\frac{y}{5}\right)=0$
Now any point on the hyperbola will be $(4 \sec \theta, 5 \tan \theta)$
length of the perpendicular from this point to the asymptotes will be
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{p}_1 & =\frac{(20 \sec \theta+20 \tan \theta)}{\sqrt{41}} \\ \& \quad \mathrm{p}_2 & =\frac{(20 \sec \theta-20 \tan \theta)}{\sqrt{41}} \therefore \quad \mathrm{p}_1 \mathrm{p}_2=\frac{400}{41}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
